channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: Bloodborne on PC remains a pipe dream, but the newly announced movie at least means taking your mouse and keyboard to the cinema is an option
item.content:encoded:
Stop shouting at me for click clacking, I'm clearly controlling the characters on this big screen

Bloodborne. It's still not on PC and doesn't look to be getting a remake which might bring it to PC any time soon. There is some good news, though. Sony have announced an animated film adaptation of the game about crimson juice and goths in hats is in the works.

The movie's been announced by Sony Pictures, who'll be making it with PlayStation Productions and Lyrical Animation as co-producers. Also co-producing is YouTuber Seán 'Jacksepticeye' McLoughlin, whom I know best via clips of him shouting at a man on a segway in flash game Happy Wheels - a secondary school lunchtime staple which really should have been treated to a graphically orgasmic Unreal Engine 5 remake by now.

Bloodborne on the big screen's set to be an animated flick, though it'll have an R rating so that all of the titular blood doesn't have to be replaced by Nickelodeon-esque green goo. There aren't any details on the plot yet, but McLoughlin has taken to Reddit to emphasise that he'll be endeavouring to make sure it's very Bloodborney. "I am going to do everything in my power to make this the BEST Bloodborne adaptation possible," he wrote. "Not only is it my favourite game ever made but I know how truly passionate the fans of this game are and how much hunger they have for more of it."

So yeah, he does this for you, ravenous Bloodborne horde. If nothing else, the movie's existence is proof that Bloodborne can be delivered to places that aren't Sony's consoles if the company are willing. Actually, on second thought, I'm not sure if that's reassuring or infuriating if you've been yearning for an official version of the game on PC for years now.

To be fair, there are always mods and plenty of other Bloodborney indie projects to look into if you're desperate to get a taste of the game on our game box of choice.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/bloodborne-on-pc-remains-a-pipe-dream-but-the-newly-announced-movie-at-least-means-taking-your-mouse-and-keyboard-to-the-cinema-is-an-option
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Krafton are no longer listed as Subnautica 2's publishers on Steam and the Epic store, following a quiet switch
item.content:encoded:
Though, their name does still appear in a different section on the game's Steam listing

Krafton have quietly disappeared from the publisher sections of Subnautica 2's Steam and Epic Games store pages. The changes appear to be recent, with SteamDB suggesting the former was made just last week to that platform's listing. Developers Unknown Worlds - now once again operating under CEO Ted Gill, amid long-running legal action between parent company Krafton and three current and former studio leads - are now cited as the game's sole publishers.

Yep, in the latest twist to the Subnautica 2 saga, if you look at its Steam and Epic pages as of writing, you'll find no mention of Krafton in the publisher sections, as was previously the case.

Judging by SteamDB's update history for the listing on Valve's storefront, the change took place on April 7th. It's worth noting that while it's seen Krafton's name pulled from the publisher section of the listing, their name looks to have been added to the 'Franchise' section at the same time, meaning the company haven't been totally removed. It's tougher to tell when the Epic listing was changed, but as of March 9th it still listed Krafton in the publisher section.

I've asked Krafton for comment. As of right now, it's unclear whether these quiet switches mean Krafton and Unknown Worlds' publishing relationship has changed, or if they're linked in any way to the long-running legal battle with original Subnautica 2 studio leads Gill, Charlie Cleveland, and Max MacGuire.

As mentioned earlier, Gill has recently been reinstated as Unknown Worlds CEO. The legal wranglings between the two sides erupted when Krafton fired the trio last year, accusing them of abandoning their duties on the game. Cue lots of lawyering over whether and when Subnautica 2 might be ready for an early access launch, with a substantial cash bonus from Krafton to Unknown Worlds tied to how well the game does out of the gate.

We'll have to see if these store changes are just routine admin or something more noteworthy. In the interim, I can at least thank them for leading me to discover that someone's tried to switch Sub 2's "choices matter" user tag on Steam to "hentai" twice within the past five days, only for it to be hastily switched back.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/krafton-no-longer-listed-as-subnautica-2-publishers-steam-epic-store-quiet-switch
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
